description: Trigger discrimination for the Phase 6 reliability cluster: hands-on observability operation vs SLO design vs incident procedures, and against the shipped audit-log-architect and systematic-debugger.

cases.prompt: Add tracing across the payment path, fix the readiness probe that always returns 200, and wire the new latency dashboard with its alert.
cases.reason: Instrumenting, fixing health checks, and wiring dashboards/alerts is the operator's hands-on territory — implementation, not reliability design or live diagnosis.

cases.prompt: Derive error budgets for our three core user journeys and define which burn rates should page.
cases.reason: Error budgets and paging policy are the reliability DESIGN; the operator implements the resulting alert spec.

cases.prompt: We need tamper-evident, tenant-scoped logging of every admin action and data export for compliance.
cases.reason: Audit events carry integrity/retention/access semantics telemetry does not — audit-log-architect designs that trail; the operator keeps telemetry and audit separate.

cases.prompt: The export service started failing intermittently an hour ago — figure out why.
cases.reason: Diagnosing an active failure is the debugger's reproduce→isolate casework; the operator builds the visibility that diagnosis uses.

cases.prompt: Document what on-call should do when the paging alert fires — triage, escalation, comms.
cases.reason: The human procedure behind the page is the incident runbook; the operator wires the alert and links to that runbook.
